title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Python Programming Variables, data types, and basic
operations,
Control Structures and Functions in Python Conditionals, loops, functions,
modules, and packages

title: Module 2
content:
Introduction to MATLAB Environment, MATLAB syntax, variables, and
arrays, MATLAB Scripts and Functions, Writing scripts, creating
functions, and plotting

title: Module 3
content:
Robot Kinematics and Dynamics, Understanding robot movement and
dynamics, Sensor Integration in Robotics, Reading sensor data, interfacing
with hardware

title: Module 4
content:
Simulation Techniques for Robotics, Building computational models,
simulating robot behaviors
Project based learning (8 Hrs)
 The objective of Project based learning to strengthen the understanding of student’s
fundamentals through effective application of theoretical concepts.  Projects can help to boost
student’s skills and widen the horizon of their thinking.
 The ultimate aim of an engineering student is to resolve a problem by applying theoretical
knowledge.
 In the beginning, students shall be given an awareness and/or guideline on how to implement and
learn this course through a project and form groups in the first few weeks.
 Each group should identify a project that can be done to understand a part of the contents of this
course and perform a literature survey.
 Choose a suitable platform for your application and the input output devices to be interfaced.
 Develop the block diagram and present before the jury within first month after the commencement
of classes.
 Implement necessary hardware and software to develop the prototype.
 Prepare a report and present before the jury for final evaluation.
